ddls:
CREATE TABLE "beers" (
  "beer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each beer */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the beer */,
  "brewery_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the brewery that produces the beer */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of beer (e.g., IPA, Stout) */,
  "abv" REAL /* Alcohol by volume percentage */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the beer's flavor and characteristics */,
  "image_url" TEXT /* URL to an image of the beer */,
  "ibc" REAL /* International Bitterness Units (IBU) of the beer */,
  "srm" REAL /* Standard Reference Method (SRM) color value of the beer */,
  "brew_date" TEXT /* Date the beer was brewed */,
  "availability" TEXT /* Availability of the beer (e.g., seasonal, limited edition, year-round) */,
  "serving_temperature" REAL /* Recommended serving temperature of the beer */,
  "food_pairing" TEXT /* Suggested food pairings that complement the beer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("beer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_beers_brewery_id FOREIGN KEY ("brewery_id") REFERENCES breweries ("brewery_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "breweries" (
  "brewery_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each brewery */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the brewery */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the brewery */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website URL of the brewery */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the brewery was founded */,
  "number_of_employees" INTEGER /* Number of employees at the brewery */,
  "tour_information" TEXT /* Information about brewery tours (availability, pricing, etc.) */,
  "social_media_links" TEXT /* Links to the brewery's social media profiles */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("brewery_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "beer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the beer being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who submitted the review */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text content of the review */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was submitted */,
  "overall_rating" REAL /* Overall rating given to the beer */,
  "rating_count" INTEGER /* Number of individual ratings given (look, smell, taste, feel) */,
  "is_flagged" INTEGER /* Whether the review has been flagged for moderation */,
  "moderation_status" TEXT /* Status of the review if it has been flagged (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_beer_id FOREIGN KEY ("beer_id") REFERENCES beers ("beer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "join_date" TEXT /* Date when the user joined the platform */,
  "display_name" TEXT /* Display name shown to other users */,
  "profile_picture_url" TEXT /* URL to the user's profile picture */,
  "bio" TEXT /* Short bio or introduction about the user */,
  "notification_preferences" TEXT /* Notification preferences (e.g., email, push notifications) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating entry */,
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the review associated with the ratings */,
  "look" REAL /* Rating for the beer's appearance */,
  "smell" REAL /* Rating for the beer's aroma */,
  "taste" REAL /* Rating for the beer's taste */,
  "feel" REAL /* Rating for the beer's mouthfeel */,
  "overall_rating" REAL /* Overall rating given to the beer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_review_id FOREIGN KEY ("review_id") REFERENCES reviews ("review_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "followers" (
  "follower_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each follow relationship */,
  "followed_user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who is following another user */,
  "follow_date" TEXT /* Date when the follow relationship was established */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("follower_id", "followed_user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user organizing the event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the event */,
  "event_date" TEXT /* Date of the event */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location where the event is held */,
  "event_type" TEXT /* Type of event (e.g., tasting, festival, workshop) */,
  "ticket_price" REAL /* Price of tickets for the event */,
  "max_participants" INTEGER /* Maximum number of participants for the event */,
  "event_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the event */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_events_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "notification_text" TEXT /* Text content of the notification */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Whether the notification has been read by the user */,
  "date_created" TEXT /* Date when the notification was created */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., new review, new follower, event update) */,
  "action_url" TEXT /* URL that the user can click to take action related to the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "analytics" (
  "analytics_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each analytics entry */,
  "beer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the beer being analyzed */,
  "total_reviews" INTEGER /* Total number of reviews for the beer */,
  "average_rating" REAL /* Average rating of the beer based on reviews */,
  "most_common_review_words" TEXT /* Most common words used in reviews for the beer */,
  "sentiment_score" REAL /* Sentiment score based on the overall tone of the reviews */,
  "rating_distribution" TEXT /* Distribution of ratings (e.g., number of 1-star, 2-star, etc.) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("analytics_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_analytics_beer_id FOREIGN KEY ("beer_id") REFERENCES beers ("beer_id")
)
